author: Andy Ho
description: Turn dictated or pasted notes into a linked, tagged knowledge graph, right inside Obsidian - uses Claude Code (your subscription) or a direct API key from Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, or a local model, your choice. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Andy Ho
manifest.description: Turn dictated or pasted notes into a linked, tagged knowledge graph - uses Claude Code (your subscription) or a direct API key from Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, or a local model, your choice.
manifest.name: Nous

name: Nous

releases.author: AndyMDH
releases.description:
Fixes the remaining community plugin review findings:

- child_process/fs/os/path are now loaded dynamically (gated behind the existing desktop/macOS checks) instead of statically - the static imports could break the plugin bundle on mobile load.
- Fixed all 22 sentence-case UI text findings.
- Vault.delete() -> FileManager.trashFile(), hardcoded ".obsidian" -> vault.configDir, setTimeout -> window.setTimeout, unnecessary type assertions, non-Error promise rejections, deprecated builtin-modules dependency removed.
- Migrated the settings tab from deprecated display() to getSettingDefinitions().
- Fixed unsafe-any JSON parsing in the LLM provider clients.

Requires Obsidian 1.13.0+ (up from 1.5.7), needed for getSettingDefinitions and FileManager.trashFile.

releases.author: AndyMDH
releases.description:
Fixes the automated Community Plugin review failures from 2.0.0:

- Removed "Obsidian" from the manifest description (redundant, flagged as an error)
- Bumped minAppVersion to 1.5.7 to match actual API usage (Vault.getFolderByPath/getFileByPath)
- Replaced raw HTML headings with Setting().setHeading()
- Replaced direct style assignment with setCssStyles()

No functional/behavior changes.

releases.author: AndyMDH
releases.description: Voice-capture ribbon button; fixed meeting-capture button re-showing QuickRecorder's window.

releases.author: AndyMDH
releases.description:
- **GLM (Z.ai) provider**: pick GLM in settings, paste a Z.ai API key — glm-5.2, glm-5.2 1M-context, and glm-5 in the model dropdown (custom ids supported).
- New developer docs: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (layer model) and docs/TECHNICAL.md (code map).
- README: technical How-it-works section; meeting-capture example hardened (settle-before-transcribe race fix, transcript filename collisions).

releases.author: AndyMDH
releases.description:
- One way in per capture type: voice = the hotkey/palette command, text+files = quick capture. The modal's duplicate Record button is gone.
- Removed the process-inbox ribbon icon (auto-processing makes it noise; the command remains). One Cortex icon in the ribbon: ➕ quick capture.

releases.author: AndyMDH
releases.description: - **Hotkey voice capture**: bind "Cortex: Toggle voice capture" to any key (Settings → Hotkeys). Press, talk, press again — the note appears enriched with the recording embedded. No modal, no clicks.

releases.author: AndyMDH
releases.description:
- Record voice notes directly in Quick capture: 🎙️ Record → talk → Stop → Capture. No core plugin, no moving files.
- Voice docs in the README simplified to the three-step flow.

releases.author: AndyMDH
releases.description:
- New logo and redesigned README visuals.
- Codebase-wide comment cleanup (minimal, constraint-bearing comments only). No functional changes from 1.4.0.

releases.author: AndyMDH
releases.description:
- **Voice capture, no extra apps**: drop any audio file (.m4a/.webm/.mp3/.wav/.ogg/.flac) in the inbox — including recordings from Obsidian's built-in Audio recorder, desktop or mobile. Cortex transcribes it (Gemini or OpenAI key) and enriches the transcript, with the original recording embedded in the note. Works in every execution mode, including CLI.
- **First-run setup wizard**: pick Claude Code or an API key, test the connection, and optionally watch a sample note get enriched — no settings page required. Re-run anytime via "Cortex: Open setup wizard".
- **Quick capture**: new ➕ ribbon icon / command — type, paste, or attach an image/PDF/audio, one click to the inbox.
- Model setting is a dropdown of current models per provider (since 1.3.0); API key field masked + Test connection button (1.3.1, first released here).

releases.author: AndyMDH
releases.description:
- Model setting is now a dropdown of current models per provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini) with a Custom escape hatch — no more typing model ids by hand. Local stays free-text (any Ollama tag).
- README: simplified for beginners, added pipeline/settings/capture visuals, dictation capture got its own section.

releases.author: AndyMDH
releases.description:
Multi-modal capture: drop a photo (whiteboard, screenshot) into the inbox alongside your usual text/voice captures and Cortex enriches it the same way - tagged, summarized, with the image embedded in the resulting note.

Works in both CLI mode (Claude Code reads the image directly) and Direct API key mode (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, and Local all supported, each with their own image content-block format).

Supported formats: png, jpg/jpeg, webp. Not supported: HEIC, GIF, multiple images per note, or resizing large images - see README for details.

See README for setup instructions.

releases.author: AndyMDH
releases.description:
Adds support for OpenAI, Gemini, and local models (e.g. Ollama) as alternatives to Anthropic in Direct API key mode - pick a provider in Settings, no code changes needed. Local mode makes no remote API call at all.

CLI mode is unaffected - it always uses Claude Code regardless of this setting.

Also simplified the README's day-to-day usage section down to one recommended path instead of presenting manual and hands-free capture as co-equal options.

See README for setup instructions.

releases.author: AndyMDH
releases.description:
Fixes CLI execution mode: `execFile` left the child process's stdin open, so `claude` waited on it before timing out and exiting nonzero — every real inbox run in CLI mode was silently failing before this fix.

If you're on Direct API key mode, this release doesn't affect you.

See README for setup instructions.

releases.author: AndyMDH
releases.description:
Initial public release.

Two execution modes, switchable in Settings → Cortex:
- Claude Code CLI (default) - shells out to `claude -p`, uses your existing subscription/CLI auth, no separate billing.
- Direct API key - the plugin calls the Anthropic API itself, works on mobile, needs a separate Anthropic API key.

See the README for the full feature list, privacy/permissions disclosure, and setup instructions.
